feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: With uncertainty around H-1B visa fee, U.S. businesses unsure how to move forward
entry.content: Earlier this week, a federal judge invalidated the White House's $100,000 H-1B fee policy in response to a lawsuit brought by 20 states.

entry.title: Why Asian Currencies Are Taking a Beating, in Charts
entry.content: Governments and central banks are battling the effects of the energy crisis, U.S. interest rates and AI spending.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
You will live long enough to open many fortune cookies.
Lucky Numbers: 16, 91, 79, 65, 13, 75

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use CTRL+CLICK to open internet pages in a new tab. SHIFT+CLICK to open in a new window.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Grapes explode when you put them in the microwave.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Beverly Sills
entry.content: “Anger begins with folly, and ends with repentance.” — Beverly Sills

entry.title: Fans anticipating critical Game 5 of NBA Finals in San Antonio
entry.content: Game 5 of the NBA Finals is in San Antonio on Saturday, and the big question is whether the Knicks clinch or the Spurs survive. Game 4 was the most-watched game ever on social media with 3 billion views and counting. Doug Williams reports from San Antonio.

entry.title: Business owner says his company relies on skilled foreign worker visas
entry.content: This week, a federal judge struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The much higher fee was intended to prevent foreign workers from taking American jobs. But some business owners say the U.S. depends on that talent. Shanelle Kaul reports.

entry.title: Royal family watches Royal Air Force flyover for king's official birthday
entry.content: The Royal family gathered on the balcony at Buckingham Palace on Saturday to greet the crowds and watch a Royal Air Force flyover in celebration of the king. Holly Williams has more from London.

entry.title: U.S. cruises past Paraguay 4-1 in World Cup opener
entry.content: The U.S. men's national soccer team made easy work of Paraguay in its World Cup opener Friday, defeating the South American side 4-1 at SoFi Stadium.

entry.title: U.S. Poised to Lose Measles Elimination Status as Utah Outbreak Nears One-Year Mark
entry.content: The spread of the highly contagious disease persists at levels not seen in the U.S. since the early 1990s.

entry.title: A woman's hypothermia death in Pittsburgh after her release from ICE custody is ruled a homicide
entry.content: The death of a woman from Haiti seeking asylum in the U.S. who died from hypothermia days after her release from federal custody was ruled a homicide by a Pennsylvania county medical examiner's office

entry.title: Trump names James M. McDonald to lead powerful New York federal prosecutor's office
entry.content: President Donald Trump says he will appoint one of his personal lawyers to serve as the top federal prosecutor in Manhattan after the attorney who holds the job was tapped this week for the role of director of national intelligence

entry.title: Trump names James M. McDonald to lead powerful New York federal prosecutor's office
entry.content: President Trump said Saturday that he will appoint one of his personal lawyers to serve as the top federal prosecutor in Manhattan after the attorney who holds the job was tapped this week for the role of director of national intelligence.

entry.title: The Iranian Enforcer Driving Tehran’s Hard Bargaining With the U.S.
entry.content: The pugilistic new paramilitary commander is seen as a power broker who is asserting control over negotiations even as the two sides appear to be near a deal.

entry.title: Indiana man's $100,000 lottery ticket validated at $20 in printing error
entry.content: An Indiana man who scratched what appeared to be a $100,000 prize from a new state lottery game was informed it had been validated at only $20, as the Hoosier Lottery acknowledged a printing error, suspended sales of the game and said it is reviewing how to resolve affected tickets.

entry.title: Lightning strikes plane flying from London to Charlotte, pilot makes emergency landing
entry.content: A plane slated to fly from London to Charlotte, North Carolina, had to double back and make an emergency landing after being struck by lightning.

entry.title: Trump Picks Sullivan & Cromwell Partner as Top Manhattan Federal Prosecutor
entry.content: James McDonald, one of the president’s personal lawyers, would replace Jay Clayton, who has been nominated as national intelligence director.

entry.title: Trump administration will not set nationwide environmental requirements, Zeldin says
entry.content: EPA CHIEF ZELDIN: No nationwide environmental standards for artificial intelligence data centers will be implemented by the Trump admin.

entry.title: OpenAI hit with multistate probe into possible user harm as its IPO looms
entry.content: OpenAI received a subpoena from several states as part of a probe into the safety of users of its chatbot as it prepares to offer stock to the public for the first time.

entry.title: U.S. agents at border near Buffalo find 1,600 pounds of marijuana
entry.content: Customs and Border Protection agents working at a border crossing near Buffalo, New York, found over 1,600 pounds of marijuana in a purported shipment of chocolate.

entry.title: A woman's hypothermia death in Pittsburgh after her release from ICE custody is ruled a homicide
entry.content: A medical examiner has ruled the death of a Haitian asylum seeker after being released from federal custody a homicide. An attorney representing her family said he expects her relatives to sue Immigration and Customs Enforcement in connection with her death.

entry.title: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report
entry.content: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report

entry.title: Unhealthy air quality in California city as huge warehouse fire burns for third day
entry.content: Officials in a Northern California city urged people sensitive to smoke to keep indoors as firefighters spent a third day Saturday battling a fire in a huge medical equipment warehouse.

entry.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal to be signed Sunday, Trump says
entry.content: President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened "immediately," once the memorandum of understanding is signed.

entry.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
entry.content: It's not clear which states are involved, but they're asking about everything from OpenAI's ad policies to its handling of health data.

entry.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center, court filing says
entry.content: A federal appellate court denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center on Friday.

entry.title: 6/13: Saturday Morning
entry.content: The White House is readying for Sunday's UFC event as questions remain over peace negotiations with Iran. Plus, the U.S. dominated Paraguay 4-1 in the team's first World Cup game.

entry.title: This thin under-pillow speaker helped me fall asleep without earbuds
entry.content: I’ve struggled with insomnia since I was very young. Like many chronic overthinkers, I tend to fall asleep best when my mind is occupied by something else, such as podcasts, YouTube compilations, or my personal favorite: rain sounds. But earbuds can be uncomfortable, and playing audio out loud isn’t exactly considerate when I’m staying at […]

entry.title: D.C.'s hottest food spot wants you to feel like you're at a dinner party
entry.content: Jon Sybert and Jill Tyler opened up Rye Bunny in the Adams Morgan neighborhood of Washington, D.C., after closing their acclaimed Michelin-starred restaurant, Tail Up Goat, when its lease ended. "CBS Saturday Morning" goes inside one of the city's most popular spots for an experience akin to a dinner party.

entry.title: A sportswriter and TV writer on what it means to be a sports fan
entry.content: Sportswriter Joe Posnanski and television writer Michael Schur were brought together by their love of sports, so they decided to travel around the world to collect more stories of fandom. The two sit down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss their book, "Big Fan: Two Friends, 82,490 Miles, and the Wild, Wonderful Sports We Love."

entry.title: Full Interview: Jahmani "Hot Shot" Swanson
entry.content: Jahmani Swanson, known as "Hot Shot" sat down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to talk about playing for the Harlem Globetrotters as the organization celebrates its 100th anniversary.

entry.title: Metal grill brushes can cause serious injury. Here's how two best friends are changing that.
entry.content: Earlier this year, more than 13 million grill brushes were recalled after multiple injuries were reported from wire bristles breaking off. Firefighter Scott Mobley and Anthony Tranchida partnered up to create a bristle-free brush that uses steam to clean your grill, and "CBS Saturday Morning" gets a first-hand look at how it works just in time for Father's Day. (Sponsored by Grill Rescue)

entry.title: Harlem Globetrotters celebrate 100 wacky years
entry.content: The Harlem Globetrotters have been entertaining crowds with their comedic athletic feats for a century. "CBS Saturday Morning" speaks with current team members on what it means to be part of the iconic squad.

entry.title: Why TD Securities anticipates even bigger days ahead for SpaceX
entry.content: Peter Haynes, TD Securities' head of index and market structure, suggests SpaceX's public debut is only a small part of the larger SpaceX timeline.

entry.title: Anthropic says it has taken its latest AI models offline to comply with new export controls
entry.content: AI giant Anthropic said Friday it has taken its latest artificial intelligence models, known as Fable 5 and Mythos 5, offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent their use by foreign nationals.

entry.title: Dow Jones Futures: The Next Test For Market — And SpaceX; Five Stocks In Buy Areas
entry.content: Dow Jones Futures: The Next Test For Market — And SpaceX; Five Stocks In Buy Areas

entry.title: Paramount Skydance merger with Warner Bros. Discovery won't harm competition, consumers, DOJ says
entry.content: An investigation by the U.S. Justice Department into Paramount Skydance's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery has determined that the mammoth Hollywood media merger is not likely to harm competition in the industry or be harmful for consumers.

entry.title: Alphabet Stock Is Up Nearly 100% Over the Past Year. Is It Still a Buy?
entry.content: Alphabet Stock Is Up Nearly 100% Over the Past Year. Is It Still a Buy?

entry.title: Some people are making guns with 3D printers. A new law seeks to cancel their print jobs
entry.content: Legislation in two of the nation's most populous states could force 3D printers to come equipped with technology blocking them from making guns

entry.title: From rockets to brain implants, here's a look at Elon Musk's vast empire
entry.content: Elon Musk, the world's richest man and now first-ever trillionaire, controls a lot of different businesses.

entry.title: From rockets to brain implants, here's a look at Elon Musk's vast empire
entry.content: Elon Musk, the world’s richest man and now first-ever trillionaire, controls a lot of different businesses

entry.title: My dad lost $250K of my parents' retirement savings to a WhatsApp scam he never questioned or verified
entry.content: My dad lost $250K of my parents' retirement savings to a WhatsApp scam he never questioned or verified

entry.title: Is Vistra (VST) Still The Best Data Center Energy Stock?
entry.content: Is Vistra (VST) Still The Best Data Center Energy Stock?

entry.title: You can ignore AI giants like SpaceX, but your 401(k) won't
entry.content: You can ignore AI giants like SpaceX, but your 401(k) won't

entry.title: 4 things to know about the new sunscreen ingredient the FDA approved
entry.content: The Food and Drug Administration approved a new sunscreen ingredient in the U.S. for the first time in 20 years. It's been used for decades in Europe and Asia.

entry.title: Some quarantined cruise ship passengers push to return home
entry.content: Some of the 18 cruise ship passengers in quarantine after a deadly hantavirus outbreak have had a tough time in isolation.

entry.title: Calder Cup playoffs schedule 2026: Bracket, times, TV channels, live streams, scores for AHL hockey postseason
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know to watch as a new Calder Cup champion is crowned in 2026.

entry.title: Where to watch Kelly Cup Finals 2026: Schedule, times, channels, live streams, scores for ECHL hockey series
entry.content: The ECHL's two dominant teams all season, Kansas City and Florida, will face off for the Kelly Cup. Here's when and where to watch every game.

entry.title: Where to watch Calder Cup Finals 2026: Schedule, times, channels, live streams, scores for AHL hockey series
entry.content: The Chicago Wolves and Toronto Marlies face off for one of hockey's most coveted trophies. Here's how to watch every game.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 free live stream: How to watch tournament matches without cable
entry.content: What channel is the World Cup on? Full schedule and how to watch every match from the summer tournament.

entry.title: Where to watch 24 Hours of Le Mans: TV coverage, live stream, entry list for 2026 endurance race
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about 24 Hours of Le Mans, including TV channel and streaming options for the endurance race.

entry.title: Where to watch NCAA track and field championships: Event schedule, TV coverage, live streams and more for 2026
entry.content: Here is everything you need to know about the 2026 NCAA Track and Field Championships, including the schedule of events along with television and streaming options.

entry.title: College World Series bracket 2026: Full schedule, scores, TV channels, live streams for NCAA baseball CWS
entry.content: This year's tournament in Omaha promises to be a thriller after a flurry of upsets earlier in the tournament. Here's when and where to watch every game.

entry.title: What channel is the World Cup on in USA? Complete TV schedule to watch 2026 matches on Fox
entry.content: How to watch World Cup in US: TV channel and live stream details for the full fixture schedule for the 2026 tournament.

entry.title: A plan to get lifesaving food to hungry kids was working well -- until it wasn't
entry.content: Through an innovative program, parents in Senegal had easy access to a therapeutic food that's a boon for malnourished kids. Now there are shortages. Health specialists say U.S. aid cuts are to blame.

entry.title: The FBI built its own replica small town to simulate real-world cyberattacks
entry.content: Hidden inside a building in Alabama, the FBI has created its own small town as a dedicated cyber training ground for simulating cyberattacks.

entry.title: 'Cool Ladies Club' is directed by 10 working-class women. They live up to the title
entry.content: They gave smartphones to 10 women from a working-class Indian community to make a documentary about their unseen and unheralded lives. The results are .... pretty cool.

entry.title: COMIC: How excessive heat kills and how to stay safe
entry.content: Human bodies have a natural cooling system, but it can do only so much in high temperatures and humidity. Here's the science behind how heat kills. And how to protect yourself.

entry.title: America Has a Credit Card Problem, Just Not the One You Think
entry.content: Consumers overall have manageable card loans, but growth in lending might be too slow

entry.title: How a Student’s Surefire Bet on Polymarket Went to Zero—Thanks to Fine Print
entry.content: Prediction markets issue unilateral ‘clarifications’ over disputed betting contracts, upsetting traders on the losing side of wagers.

entry.title: Argentina vs. Jordan tickets: Prices, seats, date for 2026 FIFA World Cup group match
entry.content: Argentina and Jordan will show down in Dallas for the final match of World Cup group play. Here's how to get tickets.

entry.title: Spain vs. Saudi Arabia tickets: Prices, seats, date for 2026 FIFA World Cup group match
entry.content: Spain vs. Saudi Arabia: Here's what to know about tickets, date and more for the FIFA World Cup match.

entry.title: OpenAI is facing investigation from a group of state attorneys general
entry.content: A coalition of state attorneys general is asking OpenAI for documents about its activities.

entry.title: Last time Knicks won NBA championship: Revisiting 1973 Finals roster led by Walt Frazier, Willis Reed
entry.content: The Knicks on the doorstep of snapping a 53-year title drought.

entry.title: Where to watch Alabama vs. Oklahoma: Channel, time, TV schedule, live stream for 2026 CWS baseball game
entry.content: Here's everything that you need to know about watching Alabama vs. Oklahoma, including TV and live stream information for Saturday's CWS showdown.

entry.title: Where to watch Brazil vs. Morocco live stream, TV channel, start time for World Cup Group C match
entry.content: Here's how to watch the Brazil vs. Morocco World Cup Group C Match, including live stream, TV channel, start time and more.

entry.title: How much it cost to build UFC Octagon at White House, capacity and more Freedom 250 details
entry.content: The UFC Freedom 250 is headed to the White House. Here's an insight into the cost and budgeting for this event.

entry.title: Coaching the U.S. was a big test for Mauricio Pochettino. Then his World Cup opener went ‘amazing’
entry.content: Pochettino, a 54-year-old Argentine, has been on the job for 20 months, but Friday’s game was unquestionably his biggest opportunity to make an impression on a wider fanbase tuning in to watch the U.S. for the first time.

entry.title: World Cup results 2026: Updated scores today, yesterday, and so far from 2026 FIFA tournament
entry.content: See the latest results from the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entry.title: Gio Reyna's buzzer-beating goal in opener was affirmation of larger message: The USMNT can play this game
entry.content: Gio Reyna's last-minute goal for the United States in their World Cup opener was just one more affirmation of what we'd seen throughout the night: The USMNT can play this game.

entry.title: How the U.S. and Iran Turned From Renewed War to the Cusp of Peace
entry.content: A U.S. helicopter crash spurred an escalation that led both sides to back down and negotiate.

entry.title: Kennedy Center Misses Deadline, Wants More Time to Remove Trump’s Name
entry.content: The center asked for an extension just before midnight Friday, saying it expects to have the name removed by Saturday.

entry.title: Anthropic blocks all customers' access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
entry.content: Anthropic has suspended all access to its new AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to comply with a government order citing national security concerns.

entry.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living
entry.content: Andrew Yang made a list of everything Americans overpay for — housing, food, wireless — and thinks the next startup gold rush is giving that money back.

entry.title: Trump Says U.S. Killed Venezuelan Tren de Aragua Gang Leader
entry.content: The president said the strike was coordinated with the government in Caracas.

entry.title: China 'strongly dissatisfied' with Pentagon move against top Chinese tech firms
entry.content: China 'strongly dissatisfied' with Pentagon move against top Chinese tech firms

entry.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
entry.content: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.

entry.title: Gene Shalit, beloved and bushy film critic on the 'Today' show, dies at 100
entry.content: Gene Shalit, the funnyman film critic who captured the hearts of "Today" show viewers across four decades of work, has died. He was 100.

entry.title: Trump says leader of Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang killed in US strike
entry.content: President says Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores killed in ‘swift and lethal’ military strike with help from VenezuelaThe US military has killed a leader in the Venezuelan street gang Tren de Aragua, Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, with the help of Venezuela, Donald Trump announced on Friday.“At my direction, the United States Southern Command delivered a swift and lethal kinetic strike to successfully execute Niño Guerrero, the infamous leader of Tren De Aragua, one of the most bloodthirsty Terrorist Organizations on Planet Earth,” Trump wrote on Truth Social. Continue reading...

entry.title: DGA's board backs tentative contract with major studios
entry.content: The DGA's board on Friday unaminously voted to recommend their members to approve a four-year contract with major studios.

entry.title: USA vs. Paraguay live score, result, highlights from USMNT's 2026 World Cup Group D match
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for USA vs. Paraguay in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Group D match in Los Angeles.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 opening ceremony live updates, reviews on Katy Perry and other performances in USA
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live updates and reviews from Katy Perry and every performance during the 2026 World Cup opening ceremony in Inglewood.

entry.title: Ole Miss vs. North Carolina baseball box score: Live stats from College World Series
entry.content: The Tar Heels and Rebels meet for the first time in 57 years.

entry.title: USA vs. Paraguay box score: Full stats from World Cup Group D match for USMNT
entry.content: The United States gets its World Cup underway on Friday night.

entry.title: Is Carli Lloyd is pregnant? News on former USWNT star and current analyst's expanding family
entry.content: Here's the latest on Carli Lloyd's second pregnancy and her growing family.

entry.title: Two returning Panthers facing tough NFL training camp challenges
entry.content: With OTAs ending Thursday, TE James Mitchell and DL LaBryan Ray could be pressured to keep their roster spots.

entry.title: Klay Thompson's days in Dallas are numbered, and these 3 teams should be calling
entry.content: Klay Thompson’s time in Dallas is likely over after a chaotic roster reset, but these three NBA teams could still use his legendary championship shooting.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Stay healthy. Walk a mile.
Lucky Numbers: 46, 53, 41, 36, 51, 98

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Wrap your bananas in tin foil to make them last longer.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Fish that live more than 800 meters below the ocean surface don't have eyes.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Henry Ford
entry.content: “You can't build a reputation on what you are going to do.” — Henry Ford

entry.title: 2026 World Cup of Darts: Draw, schedule, prize money and format explained
entry.content: One of the best tournaments on the darts calendar is almost here!

entry.title: Alabama seeks to execute man by lethal injection
entry.content: Alabama is seeking to execute a man with lethal injection hours after his nitrogen execution was prevented from going forward

entry.title: Kids' nonprofit in Seattle returns 20 free World Cup tickets over barring referee
entry.content: A youth-soccer nonprofit in Seattle has returned 20 free World Cup tickets to protest the U.S. decision to bar Somali referee Omar Artan from entering the country

entry.title: US Democratic lawmakers pledge to help speed up disaster recovery in Puerto Rico
entry.content: A group of U.S. Democratic lawmakers have promised Puerto Ricans that they would try to speed up the island’s sluggish recovery from destructive hurricanes and earthquakes, a process that relies heavily on federal funds

entry.title: Taiwan's opposition leader touts talks with China as necessary for peace
entry.content: Taiwan’s Beijing-friendly opposition leader says her party is committed to defense of the self-ruled island that China views as its own but that peace is possible by taking permanent secession off the table

entry.title: Treasury expands bank data-sharing rules tied to Trump immigration crackdown
entry.content: The Treasury Department has moved to involve banks more deeply in President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown

entry.title: Bystander wounded in shooting near White House still undergoing treatment
entry.content: A bystander wounded during an exchange of gunfire near the White House last month is an active-duty soldier and is still being treated for his wounds, according to the law firm he has retained
